rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to component ports; use implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` syntax within `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks following AADL scope rules.
generic_rules: Generate AGREE contracts using logical operators (`and`, `or`, `not`, `=>`) and initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations.
Relate visible outputs to visible inputs in guarantees; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in type-level contracts.
Declare constants and equations with correct AGREE scalar types (`int`, `real`, `bool`) matching the AADL model's visible identifiers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq name : type = expression;` for equation declarations and `const name : type = value;` for constant declarations.
Wrap AGREE annex content in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks, placing type-level contracts in component types and implementation-level bindings in implementations.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Deadline, Period) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Do not invent new identifiers or types not present in the visible AADL scope; use only those available in the current component.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing-related tokens (ms, us, Hz, Bytes)
Model fusion and insertion location decisions
